piece.title: Broadway Boogie Woogie
piece.notes: 45-minute suite in F Mixolydian. Static I7 harmonic field throughout — no resolution, no modulation. Structure is additive then subtractive: voices accumulate across movements I-IV, then dissolve in reverse order through V-VII. Tempo steps at movement boundaries (110 → 116 → 122 → 130) — set via Logic tempo track, not in the engine. Canon in movement III: after import, duplicate counterpoint MIDI region to a second track and offset 2 beats right. Harmonic correction (applied): original II/V implied major qualities not present in F Mixolydian (no B natural, no E natural). Replaced with five diatonic progression cells built from ii(Gm), iii°(Adim/half-dim), IV(unused here), v(Cm), vi(Dm), bVII(Eb), I(F): V1 ii-v-I (grid/return), V3 I-bVII-ii-v (accumulation/dissolve, Mondrian color-block motion), V4 ii-iii°-v-I (canon movements, diatonic passing dissonance), V5 vi-ii-v-I (peak movements, descending-fourths chain — functional-sounding but never touches a leading tone, so it drives without resolving). FIX PASS (post-mortem + Jeeves & Wooster reference): swapped walking bass -> motif bass and free -> sustain/motif across the piece. Walking bass and free-rhythm harmony were both independently moving against a concept that's supposed to hold still -- three uncoordinated layers instead of one locked-in grid. Movement I (m1_grid, sections 0-2) already had this right, which is likely why it was the most successful part of the original render. Swing must be set at the theme level (swing_ratio) -- it was entirely absent before.

sections.name: m1_grid
sections.notes: I. Grid — 4 min at 110 BPM. Bass alone. Mute melody and harmony in Logic. | Harmony fix: V1 (ii-v-I) x4 — corrected from II-V-I; diatonic minor ii/v, no borrowed majors.

sections.name: m1_grid
sections.notes: I. Grid — 4 min at 110 BPM. Bass alone. Mute melody and harmony in Logic. | Harmony fix: V1 (ii-v-I) — corrected from II-V-I. | Fix pass: rhythm/bass/harmony_rhythm changed from ('free', 'motif', 'motif') to ('motif', 'motif', 'motif') (walking bass and free rhythm/harmony were fighting the static-field concept; motif bass + sustain harmony + motif rhythm keep everything locked to the same swung ostinato).

sections.name: m1_grid
sections.notes: I. Grid — 4 min at 110 BPM. Bass alone. Mute melody and harmony in Logic. | Harmony check: already diatonic (I-v7-ii-v7), no change needed. | Fix pass: rhythm/bass/harmony_rhythm changed from ('free', 'motif', 'sustain') to ('motif', 'motif', 'sustain') (walking bass and free rhythm/harmony were fighting the static-field concept; motif bass + sustain harmony + motif rhythm keep everything locked to the same swung ostinato).

sections.name: m2_accumulation
sections.notes: II. Accumulation — 5 min at 110 BPM. Bass + offbeat comping stabs. Mute melody in Logic. | Harmony fix: V3 (I-bVII-ii-v) x3 — bVII as Mondrian color block, diatonic, no functional pull. | Fix pass: rhythm/bass/harmony_rhythm changed from ('free', 'walking', 'free') to ('motif', 'motif', 'sustain') (walking bass and free rhythm/harmony were fighting the static-field concept; motif bass + sustain harmony + motif rhythm keep everything locked to the same swung ostinato).

sections.name: m3_canon_entry
sections.notes: III. Canon (entry) — melodic cell arrives. Duplicate counterpoint MIDI region, offset 2 beats right for the canon effect. | Harmony fix: V4 truncated (ii-iii°-v) — diminished passing chord leads into canon entry, deliberately unresolved. | Fix pass: rhythm/bass/harmony_rhythm changed from ('free', 'walking', 'free') to ('motif', 'motif', 'sustain') (walking bass and free rhythm/harmony were fighting the static-field concept; motif bass + sustain harmony + motif rhythm keep everything locked to the same swung ostinato).

sections.name: m3_canon_full
sections.notes: III. Canon (full) — both voices in flight. | Harmony fix: V4 (ii-iii°-v-I) x3 — diatonic passing motion under full canon. | Fix pass: rhythm/bass/harmony_rhythm changed from ('free', 'walking', 'free') to ('motif', 'motif', 'sustain') (walking bass and free rhythm/harmony were fighting the static-field concept; motif bass + sustain harmony + motif rhythm keep everything locked to the same swung ostinato).

sections.name: m4_peak_build
sections.notes: IV. Full Grid (build) — tempo steps to 116 in Logic tempo track at this section boundary. | Harmony fix: V5 truncated (vi-ii-v) — descending-fourths build, withholds I into the hold section. | Fix pass: rhythm/bass/harmony_rhythm changed from ('free', 'walking', 'free') to ('motif', 'motif', 'sustain') (walking bass and free rhythm/harmony were fighting the static-field concept; motif bass + sustain harmony + motif rhythm keep everything locked to the same swung ostinato).

sections.name: m4_peak_hold
sections.notes: IV. Full Grid (hold) — all voices sustained at peak. | Harmony fix: V5 (vi-ii-v-I) x3 — descending-fourths chain, strongest driving motion at peak density. | Fix pass: rhythm/bass/harmony_rhythm changed from ('free', 'walking', 'free') to ('motif', 'motif', 'sustain') (walking bass and free rhythm/harmony were fighting the static-field concept; motif bass + sustain harmony + motif rhythm keep everything locked to the same swung ostinato).

sections.name: m5_dissolve_full
sections.notes: V. Dissolution — tempo steps to 122. Fade counterpoint track out in Logic starting bar 60. | Harmony fix: V5 truncated (vi-ii-v) — still peak-level motion as counterpoint begins to fade. | Fix pass: rhythm/bass/harmony_rhythm changed from ('free', 'walking', 'free') to ('motif', 'motif', 'sustain') (walking bass and free rhythm/harmony were fighting the static-field concept; motif bass + sustain harmony + motif rhythm keep everything locked to the same swung ostinato).

sections.name: m5_dissolve_medium
sections.notes: V. Dissolution — counterpoint gone, melody fading. Fade melody track out in Logic. | Harmony fix: V4 (ii-iii°-v-I) x3 — mirrors Movement III harmonic language as counterpoint exits. | Fix pass: rhythm/bass/harmony_rhythm changed from ('free', 'walking', 'free') to ('motif', 'motif', 'sustain') (walking bass and free rhythm/harmony were fighting the static-field concept; motif bass + sustain harmony + motif rhythm keep everything locked to the same swung ostinato).

sections.name: m5_dissolve_sparse
sections.notes: V. Dissolution — bass + dying comping only. Mirror of Movement II. | Harmony fix: V3 truncated (I-bVII-ii) — mirrors Movement II per original section notes. | Fix pass: rhythm/bass/harmony_rhythm changed from ('free', 'walking', 'free') to ('motif', 'motif', 'sustain') (walking bass and free rhythm/harmony were fighting the static-field concept; motif bass + sustain harmony + motif rhythm keep everything locked to the same swung ostinato).

sections.name: m6_fast_sparse
sections.notes: VI. Fast Grid — tempo steps to 130. Same material as Movement II but 20 BPM faster. | Harmony fix: V3 (I-bVII-ii-v) x3 — mirrors Movement II at faster tempo. | Fix pass: rhythm/bass/harmony_rhythm changed from ('free', 'walking', 'free') to ('motif', 'motif', 'sustain') (walking bass and free rhythm/harmony were fighting the static-field concept; motif bass + sustain harmony + motif rhythm keep everything locked to the same swung ostinato).

sections.name: m6_fast_medium
sections.notes: VI. Fast Grid — melody returns but no peak density. Restraint at speed. | Harmony fix: V4 truncated (ii-iii°-v) — mirrors Movement III canon language, restrained. | Fix pass: rhythm/bass/harmony_rhythm changed from ('free', 'walking', 'free') to ('motif', 'motif', 'sustain') (walking bass and free rhythm/harmony were fighting the static-field concept; motif bass + sustain harmony + motif rhythm keep everything locked to the same swung ostinato).

sections.name: m7_return
sections.notes: VII. Return — bass alone at 130. Mute everything else. Mirror of Movement I. | Harmony fix: V1 (ii-v-I) x4 — mirrors Movement I. | Fix pass: rhythm/bass/harmony_rhythm changed from ('free', 'walking', 'sustain') to ('motif', 'motif', 'sustain') (walking bass and free rhythm/harmony were fighting the static-field concept; motif bass + sustain harmony + motif rhythm keep everything locked to the same swung ostinato).

sections.name: m7_ritard
sections.notes: VII. Coda — Logic tempo ramp 130 down to 72 over these 40 bars. No volume fade. It stops. | Harmony fix: V1 (ii-v-I) — mirrors Movement I, final grid before ritard. | Fix pass: rhythm/bass/harmony_rhythm changed from ('free', 'pedal', 'sustain') to ('motif', 'pedal', 'sustain') (walking bass and free rhythm/harmony were fighting the static-field concept; motif bass + sustain harmony + motif rhythm keep everything locked to the same swung ostinato).
